title: Geblera tenuifolia
intro: Geblera tenuifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Geblera tenuifolia include alkaloids, glycosides, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Geblera tenuifolia is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in the form of essential oils and topical creams.
taxonomy: Geblera tenuifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Geblera, which is part of the order Magnoliales. Geblera tenuifolia is native to the tropical regions of Madagascar, where it can be found growing in humid forests. The classification of Geblera tenuifolia places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
